msg: PRG-06 shepherd primed and holding for the ADR-0020/0021/0022 gate release. Read shepherd-protocol + execution-tracker + executor scaffold. Armed inbox watcher (inbox.jsonl) + WS-18 Cosmo stage monitor. WS-18 baseline: WI-765 Backlog (WP-1 enumeration, order 1), WI-586 Ready (WP-TAIL, blocked-by WP-1), WI-631/632 Captured (absorbed provenance). Reviewer live-coverage of WS-18 to be confirmed at release (reviewer launches at gate-clear per tracker §6). Not claiming/refining WP-1 until inbox directive 'ADR gate cleared — proceed' or direct operator go.

PR #1183 (WI-765) run-smoke check is RED but adjudicated AMBIENT + NON-GATING. Root cause: staging DB in broken post-DROP state — legacy 'accounts' table dropped, seed/API code still reads it → Playwright seed 500 'relation accounts does not exist'. This is the exact dovetail condition PRG-06 exists to fix; NOT PR-caused (PR adds pre-graph 401 fix + docs enumeration only — touches neither seed nor accounts). run-smoke is NOT a branch-protection required check (required = main / Playwright web smoke / API Quality Gate / Merge completeness check; latter 3 GREEN, main pending). Already owned by the lane's terminal data half (WI-586 + cutover re-run/staging reset) — not filing a duplicate WI, not fixing inline. LANE-WIDE IMPLICATION: staging-dependent non-required smokes will stay red for every PRG-06 PR until the cutover is re-run; lane merges proceed on the 4 required checks + green claude-review, NOT on run-smoke. Reviewer: do not bounce PRG-06 PRs on run-smoke.

WP-1 (WI-765) MERGED (PR #1183, squash f5d067ba8) → Stage=Reviewing; Gate-1 done, WS-18 reviewer owns Gate-2. Enumeration landed + lane SIZED (artifact wp1-reader-writer-enumeration.md §6): WP-2 (listProfilesV2 + wire GET /v1/profiles — IDOR-critical, mobile-launch gate) · WP-3 (consent→consent-v2, 30+ sites) · WP-4 (deletion+export) · WP-5 (settings-v2 + verifyPersonOwnershipV2) · WP-6 (family builds: family-bridge/access/dashboard/notifications/nudge-v2) · WP-7 (billing remainder) · WP-8 (Inngest scans) · WP-9 (test-seed → test-seed-v2; gates the cutover re-run — it is the live cause of the ambient run-smoke red) · WP-FLAG (remove IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED) · terminal data half (WI-586). 6 class-(c) missing twins are security-sensitive/TDD (listProfilesV2 CRITICAL-IDOR + 5 HIGH guards). TWO asks: (1) RULE the deferred S4–S6 fold-in — RECOMMEND S4–S6 stays a SEPARATE track: enumeration shows ZERO nav-shell / mentor-is-the-app intersection (cutover is wholly API/data-layer, orthogonal to the nav shell). (2) CONFIRM ownership of the next structural step — shall the shepherd now instantiate WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 (off enumeration §6), or do you author them? Not blocked — WI-765 Gate-2 is proceeding; I await the ruling before the next wave. NB your tracker §5 open item (reviewer canon-wins invariant): operator confirmed reviewer SCOPE to me, but explicit canon-wins-invariant confirmation is still advisable for the WP-2..N code-to-canon reviews.

resolved by ic-orch-003: proceeding. WP authoring APPROVED → instantiating WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 off enumeration §6 (Backlog; canon-conformance baked into each AC per the operator principle — NOT relayed to the context-agnostic reviewer). S4-S6 ruled NOT folded into PRG-06 (orchestrator surfacing S4-S6's own disposition to operator). Anchors: WP-2 (listProfilesV2 IDOR/mobile-launch) + WP-9 (test-seed-v2 clears run-smoke); WI-586 stays last; 6 class-(c) twins → TDD + Opus plan-phase.

Parallelization (operator directive: parallelize all non-interdependent WPs at minimal quality risk). WAVE A dispatched in parallel now (Sonnet; wire-ups / independent infra, no security-twin novelty): WP-3 (WI-772 consent), WP-4 (WI-773 deletion/export), WP-8 (WI-777 Inngest scans), WP-9 (WI-778 test-seed→v2, clears run-smoke) — alongside WP-2 (WI-771, Opus, running) → ~5 concurrent. WAVE B HELD until WP-2 PR lands: WP-5/6/7 (WI-774/775/776) build class-(c) security twins (verifyPersonOwnershipV2 / guardianship guards / isPersonUnderSubscriptionV2) and should inherit WP-2's canonical v2-twin + ownership-scoping pattern (the only real quality interdependency). WP-FLAG + WI-586 stay last per the DAG. Each WP driven through strict Gate-1 + WS-18 reviewer Gate-2 individually.

Enumeration-accuracy caveat (surfaced by WP-8/WI-777). The WP-1 enumeration over-counted some (b) wire-up sites: the CUT-B2 lane already wired the Inngest scan/nudge/family-fanout functions to v2 (commits 9ab1076ec + 8bf67fc54, 2026-06-13, on origin/main) via an isIdentityV2EnabledInStep() helper the enumeration's branch-detection missed. Impact: WP-8 (WI-777, scans) FULLY pre-delivered (verified) → handled as a verification-PR (the v2 query path has no test coverage — a real, in-scope gap); WP-6 (WI-775, family/nudge) PARTIALLY pre-done (family-v2.ts + recall-nudge wired) → its Wave-B brief will say wire only the genuinely-unbranched remainder, do not rebuild; WP-3 consent-v2 twin exists (CUT-B2-modified) but the consent.ts callers are likely real work; WP-4/WP-7 services untouched. Net: lane somewhat smaller than the §6 sizing; no full re-audit — running executors verify actual main-state per-WP and report already-done scope, which I disposition as here.
